input.prompt:
I'm worried these tests might have problems. Audit
`Payments.Tests/PaymentProcessorTests.cs` for .NET test anti-patterns
using the full anti-pattern catalog (missing assertions, swallowed
exceptions, always-true/self-comparing assertions, flakiness, magic
values, duplicate tests, broad exception types, etc.) and tell me
what (if anything) genuinely needs fixing — be careful not to invent
issues that aren't really there.

Give me a severity-ranked list (Critical / Warning / Info / "no
issues found"). Each finding must be tied to a named anti-pattern
from the catalog and include a concrete code-level fix. Do not edit
the files — this is a read-only review.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to audit a test file for .NET test anti-patterns. Let me read the file first.

data.content:
## Audit Result: **No issues found** ✅

This test class is well-written and does not exhibit any of the standard .NET test anti-patterns:

| Anti-pattern checked | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Missing assertions | ✗ — Every test has meaningful assertions |
| Swallowed exceptions | ✗ — Exception tests use `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` correctly |
| Always-true / self-comparing assertions | ✗ — All comparisons are against expected domain values |
| Broad exception types (`Exception`) | ✗ — Asserts the specific `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` |
| Magic values without context | ✗ — Amounts/order-IDs are obvious literals in context |
| Duplicate / redundant tests | ✗ — Zero & negative are distinct boundary cases |
| Flakiness (shared state, time, randomness) | ✗ — Each test creates its own processor + fake |
| Missing Arrange/Act/Assert separation | ✗ — Clean AAA structure throughout |
| Over-mocking / testing implementation | ✗ — Uses a simple fake, asserts observable behavior |

The tests are concise, deterministic, assert specific expected outcomes, and cover both happy-path and error scenarios. No changes recommended.
